Home / Services / Light Clearing

Light Clearing

Efficient vegetation and debris removal with minimal environmental impact for your next project.

Get a Free Estimate

Overview

Whether you’re preparing a building site, reclaiming overgrown pasture, creating fire breaks, or simply opening up a view, Earthwise Excavating provides efficient light clearing that gets your land ready while respecting the natural environment.

Our conservation-focused approach means we don’t just clear everything in sight. We work with you to identify what stays and what goes, preserving mature trees, natural drainage features, and the character of your property while removing the brush, saplings, and undergrowth that’s in the way.

What’s Included

  • Property Walkthrough — We identify the clearing boundaries, note trees and features to preserve, and plan equipment access.
  • Brush & Sapling Removal — Efficient removal of undergrowth, small trees, and invasive vegetation using compact track equipment.
  • Debris Management — Cleared material is stacked, chipped, or hauled away depending on your preference and local regulations.
  • Surface Cleanup — We leave the cleared area clean and ready for its next use, not a tangled mess of stumps and debris.
  • Optional Seeding — We can seed cleared areas to stabilize soil and establish grass cover to prevent erosion.

Our Process

  1. On-Site Walkthrough — We review the area, flag trees to keep, and discuss your goals for the cleared land.
  2. Clear Estimate — You receive a written quote covering scope, timeline, and debris handling.
  3. Careful Clearing — Our operators work methodically, preserving what matters and clearing what doesn’t.
  4. Site Restoration — We grade and clean the area, and optionally seed for erosion control and a finished appearance.

FAQ

Common Questions About Light Clearing

What does light clearing include? +

Light clearing involves removing brush, saplings, small trees, undergrowth, and surface debris from a property. It's different from full timber harvesting — we focus on clearing manageable vegetation to prepare land for construction, improve access, restore pasture, or manage overgrowth while preserving desirable trees and natural features.

Will light clearing damage the soil on my property? +

When done properly, light clearing has minimal soil impact. At Earthwise, our conservation-focused approach means we use appropriately sized equipment, avoid working in wet conditions when possible, and take care to minimize soil compaction and disturbance. We can also seed disturbed areas after clearing to prevent erosion.

How much does light clearing cost per acre? +

Light clearing costs vary widely depending on vegetation density, terrain, accessibility, and what's being removed. Rather than quoting per-acre rates that may not apply to your situation, we provide free on-site estimates based on the actual conditions of your property. Call us at (513) 212-7585 to schedule a walkthrough.

Do I need permits for clearing land in Ohio? +

Permit requirements depend on the scale of clearing, your location, and whether the property is near waterways or wetlands. Some municipalities and townships have their own regulations. We can help you determine what, if any, permits are needed for your specific project during the consultation.

Ready to Get Started?

Contact us today for a free consultation about light clearing.
